description:
The literal mutator currently emits mutation sites without validating that
the resulting AST will compile or run. Two known cases:

## Case 1: Capture arguments flipped to &0

`&1`, `&2`, ... get flipped to `&0` by the literal mutator. Captures require
args numbered 1-255; `&0` fails at compile time. Counted as `compile_error`
rather than a meaningful mutant.

Reproductions:
- v1: `lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/dispatch.ex` (`&MapSet.member?(candidates, &1)`),
  also two instances in `ast.ex`
- v2 (builder, 2026-05-18 study): reproduced again

## Case 2: Range step flipped to 0

A `Range` step literal (`a..b//1` or `Range.new(a, b, 1)`) gets flipped to `0`.
`Range.new(a, b, 0)` raises `ArgumentError: cannot create range with step 0`
at runtime — the mutation makes it past compile but blows up before tests
run, so it's classified as a runtime error rather than a meaningful mutant.

Reproduction:
- Builder study (2026-05-18). Specific file location captured in the per-run
  JSON at `/tmp/builder-mutagen-runs/`.

## Why this matters

These show up as noise in the JSON output (compile_error or aborted-run
buckets), and they inflate "mutagen failure mode" counts in adopter
dashboards. They are not test gaps — they are invalid mutations the mutator
should never have emitted. The kill_rate metric ignores them, so the
correctness impact is small, but the trust signal to adopters is bad
("why is mutagen producing nonsense mutations?").

## Proposed fix

In `MutagenEx.Mutators.Literal`, when enumerating a literal site, check
whether the literal occurs inside a context where 0 is structurally invalid:

1. **Capture context.** If the parent AST node is `:&` (capture) and the
   literal is a positional integer reference, never emit `0` as a target.
2. **Range-step context.** If the literal occurs in a `..//step` AST or
   inside a `Range.new(_, _, step)` call as the third arg, never emit `0`
   as a target. Also consider banning `step` mutations that would invert
   sign without flipping the endpoints (`1..10//-1` is a valid but empty
   range — equivalent mutant territory).

General principle: pre-flight every (site, candidate) pair through a
syntactic validity check before emitting it. Mutators that emit syntactic
junk reduce confidence in the killable/survivable numbers.

## Evidence

- v1 study failure-modes section (Cases 1):
  ~/Documents/my-vault/LLMs/reports/mutagen_ex/2026-05-17-efficacy-study.md
- Builder study failure-modes section (Cases 1 and 2):
  ~/Documents/my-vault/LLMs/reports/builder/2026-05-18-efficacy-study.md
- Cross-project synthesis Findings 4.2 and 4.3

## Acceptance

- Test fixture: a module with `&MapSet.member?(set, &1)` and `1..10//2`
  produces zero sites for capture-arg-zero and range-step-zero mutations.
- Other literal mutations on the same expressions still work (e.g. `&1`
  in non-capture context still mutates).

comments.text:
ORCHESTRATOR: scope annotation (no implementation yet)

Allowed touches:
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal/**.ex (if helpers carved out)
- test/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal_test.exs
- test/fixtures/**/*.ex (new fixture with captures/ranges allowed)
- .spec/specs/mutators.spec.md (only if a requirement must be recorded)

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/*.ex (other mutators — do not touch)
- lib/mutagen_ex/ast.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/**
- lib/mutagen_ex/cli.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/json_*.ex

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not change mutator dispatch / registration logic.
- Do not refactor other mutators while in this file.
- Existing non-capture / non-range literal mutations must keep producing the same sites.
- No new external runtime deps.

Verification command: mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test

WORKER_ERROR
Session: codex-274c8e7b
Log: /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-literal-mutator-validate/.codex-runs/mutagen-z9k.log

Reason: The scope annotation is too narrow for the requested fix. `MutagenEx.Mutators.Literal.match?/1`, `mutate/1`, and `validate/1` receive only the current AST node or swapped AST node; the invalid cases require knowing the parent/context (`{:&, _, [n]}`, `{:..//, _, [_, _, step]}`, and `Range.new(_, _, step)`). Bare integer child nodes are what currently produce the invalid `1 -> 0` and step `1/2 -> 0` sites, and `literal.ex` cannot distinguish those from ordinary non-capture/non-range integer literals without context from `MutationEnumerator` or dispatch traversal.

Scope conflict: Correct implementation needs an allowed change to pass parent/path context into literal validation or to let the enumerator filter context-sensitive invalid literal candidates before emitting sites. The scope annotation explicitly marks `lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex` and other dispatch/registration logic out of scope, so widening silently would violate the ticket rules.

ORCHESTRATOR: scope annotation revised (replaces prior)

Reason: the prior annotation was too narrow. The pre-validation requires
parent/path context that lives in the mutation enumerator, not in
literal.ex alone. Widening scope on this ticket rather than carving a
subticket because it is one cohesive change.

Allowed touches:
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal/**.ex (if helpers carved out)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex (newly allowed — to thread parent
  AST context into literal validation OR to filter invalid candidates
  before emission)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators.ex (newly allowed — only if mutator dispatch
  signature must change to accept context; keep the change minimal)
- test/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal_test.exs
- test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs (newly allowed — only if
  enumerator behavior changed and needs coverage)
- test/fixtures/**/*.ex (new fixture with captures/ranges allowed)
- .spec/specs/mutators.spec.md (only if a requirement must be recorded)
- .spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md (only if a requirement must
  be recorded)

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/*.ex other than literal.ex (other mutators —
  do not touch their internals)
- lib/mutagen_ex/ast.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/**
- lib/mutagen_ex/cli.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/json_*.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner.ex

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not refactor other mutators (arithmetic, boolean, etc.) — even if
  passing through.
- Existing non-capture / non-range literal mutations must keep producing
  the same sites (regression check).
- Do not change the public API of MutagenEx.Mutators dispatch beyond
  what's strictly necessary to thread context.
- No new external runtime deps.

Verification command: mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test

IMPLEMENTED
Session: codex-0fa3fe05
Log: /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-literal-mutator-validate/.codex-runs/mutagen-z9k.2.log

Commit: 6c628c1
Files touched: lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex, test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs
Tests added: MutagenEx.MutationEnumeratorTest context-sensitive literal validation (bw mutagen-z9k): capture positional args do not emit invalid &0 literal sites; range step literals do not emit invalid step-zero sites; ordinary non-capture integer literals still produce literal sites
Verification stubs flipped: none

Summary: Threaded immediate parent/index context through the mutation enumerator walker and used it to skip literal mutations that would produce &0, a ..//0 step, or Range.new(_, _, 0). Added focused enumerator coverage for capture args, range steps, and ordinary non-capture integer literals; direct elixirc/ExUnit verification passed, while required Mix commands are recorded in the log as blocked by sandbox TCP :eperm in Mix.PubSub before project compilation.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: 7209748f-d9d1-4fd8-bd5a-a127a7a27b9d
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/7209748f-d9d1-4fd8-bd5a-a127a7a27b9d.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 2
  - lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex
  - test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0
(Both files match the REVISED "Allowed touches" globs: lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex and test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs were newly admitted by the 19:09:30Z annotation. lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal.ex and lib/mutagen_ex/mutators.ex were untouched.)

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 10, passed: 10
  - mutation_enumeration.v1 (mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs): 30/30
  - mutation_enumeration.v2 (mutation_enumerator_property_test.exs): 3/3
  - mutation_enumeration.v3 (--only filtering): 3/3
  - mutation_enumeration.v4 (enumerator test, r7 cap): 30/30 (same file as v1)
  - mutation_enumeration.v5 (head_atom_dispatch_test.exs): 47/47
  - mutators.v1 (mutators_test.exs): 15/15
  - mutators.v2 (mutators/id_stability_test.exs): 4/4
  - mutators.v3 (mutators_property_test.exs): 11/11
  - mutators.v4 (mutators/ --only validate): 26/26
  - mutators.v5 (mutators/case_drop_classification_test.exs): 4/4

- Requirements coverage (must requirements relevant to this ticket):
  - mutagen.mutation_enumeration.r1 (deterministic site order): covered by mutation_enumerator_property_test.exs and existing s1-style assertions in mutation_enumerator_test.exs. Would fail if context threading reordered children — the new walk_children uses Enum.with_index then Enum.reduce, preserving left-to-right order; r9 head-atom dispatch equivalence test (47 tests, head_atom_dispatch_test.exs) would surface any reorder. Concrete failure: head_atom_dispatch byte-identity assertion would diff.
  - mutagen.mutation_enumeration.r3 (skipped list parallel to sites): covered by test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs:794 — asserts `result.skipped` contains a {mutator: :literal, reason: :structurally_invalid} entry for the capture &1 case. Would fail if contextually_invalid_literal? returned :ok instead of {:skip, :structurally_invalid}.
  - mutagen.mutation_enumeration.r9 (head-atom pre-filter order-preservation): covered by head_atom_dispatch_test.exs (47 tests). Would fail if the new context arg perturbed candidate order.
  - mutagen.mutators.r1 (match/mutate/validate triad): covered by mutators_test.exs (15 tests). Would fail if the new contextually_invalid_literal? short-circuit broke ordinary literal validate paths — but the new gate runs BEFORE mutator.validate/1 and only on (:literal, zero-target, qualifying parent), so ordinary literal sites are unaffected. test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs:842 ("ordinary non-capture integer literals still produce literal sites") asserts the regression directly. Would fail if invalid_zero_literal_context?/1 over-matched on non-capture / non-range parents.
  - mutagen.mutators.r3 (stable site IDs): covered by mutators/id_stability_test.exs (4 tests). Would fail if run_mutator's new context parameter changed how Mutators.site_id(file, node, name) is invoked — it does not; site_id is still called on the original node.

- Acceptance-criteria coverage (ticket-level):
  - "&MapSet.member?(set, &1) produces zero sites for capture-arg-zero": mutation_enumerator_test.exs:790-792 refutes any literal site with original_ast=1, mutated_ast=0 in the CaptureFixture, and asserts the parallel skipped entry exists.
  - "1..10//2 produces zero sites for range-step-zero": mutation_enumerator_test.exs:814-820 filters for step-position literal-zero sites on lines 2 and 3 (`2..10//1` and `Range.new(a, b, 1)`) and asserts the list is empty. Endpoint literal on line 4 (`1..10`) still mutates (line 822-826). Note: the fixture uses //1 and Range.new(..,..,1) rather than //2 from the ticket text; that's a stronger test because 1→0 is exactly the regression class the ticket calls out.
  - "Other literal mutations on the same expressions still work": mutation_enumerator_test.exs:842 — `OrdinaryLiteral.one/0` still emits a 1→0 literal site.

- Intent-level scope concerns: none.
  - Public Mutators dispatch API unchanged: lib/mutagen_ex/mutators.ex untouched, lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal.ex untouched. Context is threaded internally inside MutationEnumerator's private walker; the mutator behaviour callbacks (match?/1, mutate/1, validate/1) keep their 1-arity contract.
  - Other mutators untouched: only literal-flavoured sites are gated by contextually_invalid_literal?(:literal, ...); the function is a no-op for any other mutator name.
  - Existing non-capture / non-range literal sites preserved: confirmed by the third new test and by 643/643 project tests passing (including id_stability_test).
  - No new external runtime deps: diff is enumerator + test only, no mix.exs changes.

- Project verification: pass.
  - mix compile --warnings-as-errors: "Generated mutagen_ex app" — clean.
  - mix test: 643 tests, 0 failures (19 excluded by default tags). Verified from inside the stage worktree at /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-literal-mutator-validate. The Codex sandbox TCP :eperm noted in IMPLEMENTED does not apply here.

- Audit-expanded scope: revised-by-orchestrator (see "ORCHESTRATOR: scope annotation revised" comment 2026-05-18T19:09:30Z). The widened scope explicitly admits lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex and test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs — both touched files match.

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- Implementation routes through `contextually_invalid_literal?/3` in the enumerator, short-circuiting before the mutator's own validate/1. This keeps Mutators.Literal contract-pure (no new context parameter) while satisfying the ticket's structural pre-flight goal. It is one cohesive change, as the revised annotation describes.
- The capture-context guard matches only `%{parent: {:&, _, [_arg]}, index: 0}` — i.e., the single-argument form of `&` where the integer literal IS the positional reference. This is correct: a capture body like `&MapSet.member?(set, &1)` has the `&1` represented as `{:&, _, [1]}`, with the integer at index 0. Non-capture integer literals (function arity in `&Mod.fun/1`, etc.) are not gated because they appear with different ambient parents.
- The range guards cover both `..//` syntax sugar and explicit `Range.new(_, _, step)` calls — both at index 2 of their respective parent shapes. Endpoint literals (index 0/1) remain free to mutate.
- Advisory only: spec text (`.spec/specs/mutators.spec.md` r2 and the validate_predicates decision) describes `:structurally_invalid` as a `validate/1`-returned skip reason. The implementation here emits the skip from the enumerator rather than the mutator's `validate/1`. The observable JSON shape (mutation.skipped entry with reason :structurally_invalid) is identical and matches spec intent; if a future refactor pushes this into Mutators.Literal.validate/3 (taking context), the spec wording would line up more directly. Not blocking — the revised annotation explicitly allows enumerator-side filtering.

title: Literal mutator: pre-validate AST shape (Range step, capture args)
